name: Trans-Resveratrol
summary: Trans-resveratrol is the bioactive trans isomer of resveratrol studied for metabolic and vascular biomarkers. Human results are mixed and dose-dependent.
description: Trans isomer of resveratrol with SIRT1/AMPK-related mechanistic interest and mixed human metabolic findings.
safetyNotes: GI upset; possible antiplatelet activity; caution with bleeding-risk medications.
contraindications: anticoagulant or antiplatelet medication such as warfarin or clopidogrel or aspirin due to documented bleeding risk from BCRP and CYP2C9 inhibition raising warfarin exposure
high doses may inhibit CYP3A4 or CYP2D6 or CYP2C9 raising blood levels of drugs with a narrow therapeutic window such as certain antidepressants or statins or pain medications
hormone-sensitive conditions such as certain breast cancer or uterine fibroids or endometriosis due to estrogen-like activity
pregnancy or breastfeeding without clinician guidance due to insufficient safety data
